Even if you have, let's say, a 10 or 15 minute lesson, you can break that up into subparts and work on each part at a time and record it. Then take a little bit of break, start recording again. One thing to think about if you decide to do a voiceover is that if you are not on the screen, the audio is even more critical. So you want to make sure your audio is pristine. You don't want to sound muffled. You don't want to have distracting noises in the background because there's nothing else that they're paying attention to other than the words on the screen and your voice. There's no movement. There's no face. So that audio is critical. So make sure it's just on point. Also, just a tip on your slides. If your slides are coming up on the screen, you don't want to pack the slides with 12 point type that's edge to edge and then people are trying to read that.
